A01:2021.name: Broken Access Control
A01:2021.description: Restrictions on what authenticated users are allowed to do are often not properly enforced. Attackers can exploit these flaws to access unauthorized functionality and/or data.

A02:2021.name: Cryptographic Failures
A02:2021.description: Failures related to cryptography (or lack thereof) which often lead to exposure of sensitive data or system compromise. Previously known as Sensitive Data Exposure.

A03:2021.name: Injection
A03:2021.description: Untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query, leading to execution of unintended commands or unauthorized data access. Includes SQLi, NoSQLi, OS command injection, LDAP, OGNL, expression-language injection.

A04:2021.name: Insecure Design
A04:2021.description: A category about risks related to design and architectural flaws. A secure design can still have implementation defects, but an insecure design cannot be saved by perfect implementation.

A05:2021.name: Security Misconfiguration
A05:2021.description: Insecure default configurations, incomplete or ad-hoc configurations, open cloud storage, misconfigured HTTP headers, verbose error messages containing sensitive information.

A06:2021.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
A06:2021.description: Using components with known vulnerabilities. Includes EOL software, missing patches, libraries with known CVEs, and missing component-version inventories.

A07:2021.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
A07:2021.description: Confirmation of the user's identity, authentication, and session management is critical to protect against authentication-related attacks. Previously known as Broken Authentication.

A08:2021.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2021.description: Code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations. Includes insecure deserialization (formerly A08:2017), unsigned auto-updates, untrusted CI/CD pipelines.

A09:2021.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
A09:2021.description: Inadequate logging and monitoring, coupled with missing or ineffective integration with incident response, allows attackers to attack systems further, maintain persistence, pivot, and tamper or destroy data.

A10:2021.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
A10:2021.description: SSRF flaws occur whenever a web application is fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL. It allows an attacker to coerce the application to send a crafted request to an unexpected destination.
